steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Identify the area scale factor
steps.content: When two figures are similar with linear scale factor $k$, the area scales by $k^2$. Since each side of EFGH is 6 times a corresponding side of ABCD, we have $k = 6$. Therefore, the area multiplier is $6^2 = 36$.

steps.title: Calculate the new area
steps.content: The area of ABCD is 96 square units. Multiply by the area scale factor of 36: $$96 \times 36 = 3,456$$. D is correct.
